title: Kubectl user preferences (kuberc)
text:
A Kubernetes
kuberc
configuration file allows you to define preferences for
kubectl
,
such as default options and command aliases. Unlike the kubeconfig file, a
kuberc
configuration file does
not
contain cluster details, usernames or passwords.
On Linux / POSIX computers, the default location of this configuration file is
$HOME/.kube/kuberc
.
The default path on Windows is similar:
%USERPROFILE%\.kube\kuberc
.
To provide kubectl with a path to a custom kuberc file, use the
--kuberc
command line option,
or set the
KUBERC
environment variable.
A
kuberc
using the
kubectl.config.k8s.io/v1beta1
format allows you to define
the following types of user preferences:
Aliases
- allow you to create shorter versions of your favorite
commands, optionally setting options and arguments.
Defaults
- allow you to configure default option values for your
favorite commands.
Credential Plugin Policy
- allow you to configure
a policy for exec credential plugins.
